stories.headline: Protests against Jared Kushner’s Albania resort project escalate into sovereignty and corruption battle

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: BREAKING: The Protests Against Jared Kushner’s Albania Resort Project Have Escalated. What started as a fight over protected wetlands and flamingo habitats has become something much bigger. Thousands of Albanians are now framing the project as a battle over sovereignty, corruption, and whether prime coastal land should be handed to politically connected foreign billionaires. Then came the signs. “Epstein’s Friends Are Not Welcome In Albania.” Now the European Commission is warning that the controversy could affect Albania’s EU ambitions. This is no longer a local zoning dispute. It’s becoming an international political fight.

stories.headline: India freezes Starlink approvals over security concerns tied to Iran.
stories.body: India has effectively frozen approvals for Elon Musk’s space-based internet service Starlink to begin commercial operations, citing concerns over the use of its satellite terminals in the Iran war, according to people familiar.

stories.headline: Pope Leo sat with pilots on flight from Madrid to Barcelona during Spain tour.
stories.body: Pope Leo sat with the pilots of a flight from Madrid to Barcelona on the second leg of his Spain tour. The pontiff was invited aboard by one of the captains, and waved at Spanish Air Force fighter jet pilots escorting the plane

stories.headline: Federal judge voids Trump’s $100,000 H-1B visa application fee requirement.
stories.body: A federal judge has voided President Donald Trump’s requirement of a $100,000 application fee for H-1B visas, ruling that he lacked authority to impose the new policy for a program used by companies to hire highly skilled foreign workers in specialized fields.

stories.headline: President Trump formally nominated Todd Blanche as attorney general.
stories.body: Breaking News: President Trump formally nominated Todd Blanche as attorney general, setting up a confirmation fight.

stories.headline: Elon Musk agrees with post on rushed vote counting
Joe Rogan criticizes Bernie Sanders for shifting from millionaires to billionaires as the problem.
stories.body:
Joe Rogan can no longer hide how he really feels anymore after exposing to financial expert Caleb Hammer that Bernie Sanders is a total hypocrite:

ROGAN: “Bernie Sanders used to rally against millionaires, but now he’s a millionaire so he started moving the goalposts to billionaires being the problem. It’s adorable.”

HAMMER: “Even Gavin Newsom is starting to change his tune on the billionaire tax.”

ROGAN: “Yeah because all the billionaires are leaving his state. All he cares about is his personal gain. He doesn’t give any real answers to questions.”

HAMMER: “That’s true.”

ROGAN: “There’s a giant number of college kids now that think communism is the solution. They genuinely believe in it. It’s very scary.”

HAMMER: “They’re retarded. Communism has never worked anywhere ever.”

ROGAN: “These teachers and professors don’t live in the real world. They’ve never had their own business. They are brainwashing kids with ideas and concepts that don’t work at all in real life."

stories.headline: Criticism of VP's public attack on Israel and recommendation of Theo Von podcast.
stories.body: ⭕️ So the WH sends the VP to attack Israel publicly and prepare the public for what is about to be an Obama deal 2.0.  And the VP didn't even push back about the lie that Israel is spying on the US. No wonder the VP recommended Theo Von as a "good podcast" for young people. Just disgusting.

stories.headline: Sea lion pups released back into ocean in Southern California
stories.body: Crowds cheered for three sea lion pups released back into the ocean along Playa del Rey in Southern California on Monday. The trio had been malnourished and were getting help at a mammal care center.
